phase1: Map all endpoints: HTTP method + path + handler — identify missing Pydantic request models
Assess async patterns: are all I/O-bound handlers async def? Note any sync blocking calls
Document dependency injection: FastAPI Depends(), or equivalent in Flask/Django
Identify database session management: SQLAlchemy async sessions, connection pool settings
Check background task usage: Celery, FastAPI BackgroundTasks, arq — assess error handling
Review authentication/authorization: OAuth2, JWT decode, permission guards per route
phase3: Define all request/response bodies with Pydantic v2 models; never use raw dicts in handlers
Make all I/O-bound route handlers async; use asyncio-compatible DB drivers (asyncpg, aiomysql)
Inject dependencies via FastAPI Depends() — keep handler bodies under 15 lines
Raise HTTPException with explicit status codes and detail messages; use exception handlers for domain errors
Use lifespan context manager for startup/shutdown (DB connections, caches) — avoid global state
Return consistent response schemas; document all endpoints with response_model and status_code
phase4: Test endpoints with FastAPI TestClient (sync) or AsyncClient from httpx for async tests
Override dependencies in tests using app.dependency_overrides to inject mocks
Test Pydantic models directly: valid inputs, invalid inputs, edge cases with pytest.mark.parametrize
Use pytest-asyncio for async service/repository unit tests
Assert OpenAPI schema completeness: all endpoints have request/response models
